findings.evidence: The signature at lines 46-48 assumes `[Field K] [NumberField K]` and `Q : Ideal (𝓞 K)`, while the proof at lines 50-52 only rewrites `Q.under ℤ = span {(p : ℤ)}` and `Ideal.mem_span_singleton`; Mathlib defines `Ideal.under`, `Ideal.mem_under`, and `Ideal.LiesOver` for general `[Algebra A B]` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Over.lean:93-122`.
findings.fix: Generalize it to an arbitrary target algebra over `ℤ`, e.g. `{B : Type*} [Semiring B] [Algebra ℤ B] (Q : Ideal B) [Q.LiesOver (span {(p : ℤ)})]`, preferably in/near `Ideal` rather than `NumberField`, and specialize at use sites.
findings.issue: `algebraMap_int_mem_iff_dvd_of_liesOver` is public API specialized to `𝓞 K` with `[Field K] [NumberField K]`, but the statement only uses that `Q` lies over `span {(p : ℤ)}`.

summary: The earlier pointwise helper generality issues are fixed, but the new public `LiesOver` membership lemma is stated at a number-field-specific level while its proof is purely ideal-theoretic.
